methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $120M total raised proxy.

methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Riverlane, a Series C quantum computing company founded by Steve Brierley, is evaluated against a sector comparison universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given insufficient narrow peer clustering. Using $120M in total capital raised as a proxy baseline, a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement yields an implied valuation of $204M. This method is grounded in observed venture capital progression patterns at this stage. Peer-median analysis was not applicable: while seven quantum computing companies exist in the broader peer set, none satisfied clustering criteria for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with sufficient sample size (minimum three required). Applying an all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the nascency of quantum computing venture valuations.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $173M to $235M, centered on $204M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.7x multiplier—which assumes typical risk and growth profiles—and the limited applicability of public comps trading at varied maturity levels and market conditions. Riverlane's specific technical position within error correction and software layers, relative to the broader quantum stack, remains a material but unquantified variable within this range.
